recognized_artifact: Incident Command System (ICS) — standard emergency/disaster management organizational framework

branches.condition: whether the change is situational (re-observe) or structural (re-allocate roles)

candidate_missing_primitives: Delegate (assigning authority downward through a chain, distinct from Allocate which assigns resources/tasks)
Escalate (passing a decision or resource request upward through a hierarchy)
ambiguities: 'Organize roles and responsibilities' maps loosely to 'Allocate' but could also be read as a one-time 'Bound' activity rather than a recurring step
'Tactical priorities' decision could map to either 'Prioritize' or 'Decide'; chose 'Decide' since the text frames it as a single determination rather than an ongoing ranking process

No explicit 'Verify' or 'Evaluate' primitive appears in the source text, despite being central to most real ICS implementations (e.g., after-action review) — likely a gap in this particular blinded excerpt rather than the framework itself
notes: Sequence reflects a single command cycle (plan → communicate → decide → coordinate → reassess) with reassessment feeding back into either re-observation of conditions or re-allocation of roles, depending on whether the trigger is environmental or organizational. The lack of an explicit Verify/Evaluate primitive in the source text is notable since real ICS practice includes structured evaluation (e.g., after-action review).
